basics.name: Shantanu Nitin Ghodgaonkar
basics.label: Mechatronics, Robotics and Automation Engineer
basics.summary: A Mechatronics, Robotics, and Automation Engineering graduate student at NYU with a strong background in robotics fundamentals, control theory, software development, and embedded systems. Experience includes serving as an Adjunct Professor, Associate Software Engineer, and engineering intern, working on advanced robotics simulations, automotive diagnostic tools, and IoT devices.

work.name: NYU Tandon School of Engineering
work.summary: Instructing Automatic Control Laboratory and developing advanced control algorithms for a 7-DOF hexapod robot.
highlights: Taught PC-based data acquisition, PID & LQR Control, and real-time control systems.
Guided student projects on the Quanser Q8-USB H.I.L. control board and Simulink.
Conducted workshops for high school students on electronics and embedded systems (Arduino Uno).
Spearheaded development of control algorithms for a 7-DOF hexapod robot using numerical optimization.
Implemented tripod gait via Kinematic Trajectory Optimization with a custom SQP Solver.
Utilized MuJoCo in CoppeliaSim for validating precomputed gait patterns.
Working on Contact-Rich Model Predictive Control for dynamic trajectory optimization.
Preparing to develop an Unscented Kalman Filter for state estimation under non-Gaussian uncertainty.

work.name: Bosch Global Software Technologies
work.summary: Developed Java-based automotive diagnostic tools, improving efficiency and reducing lead times.
highlights: Developed and maintained Java-based Automotive Diagnostic Tools for ODX data processing.
Built an ANTLR-based A2L parser for Daimler, completed 4 months ahead of schedule.
Led development of a customized automation tool for INEOS, reducing development time by 70%.
Created internal tools that sped up ODX error resolution by 40%.
Verified ODX data and conducted ECU simulation testing for McLaren using OTX.
Supported development of HTML, CSS, and jQuery-based screens for Bosch GRADE-X.
Authored diagnostic content for FOTA product prototyping.
Managed Agile sprints with Jira, achieving a 90% on-time delivery rate.
Served as a Technical Interviewer and produced a training video library, reducing onboarding time by 30%.

work.name: Bosch Global Software Technologies
work.summary: Developed OTX screens and integrated them into ECU simulation systems.
highlights: Created OTX screens using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript for ECU simulation.
Integrated and validated OTX screens for vehicle testing in diagnostic systems.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to improve ECU simulation accuracy.

work.name: FluxGen Sustainable Technologies
work.summary: Developed wireless monitoring and wearable devices to improve remote healthcare solutions.
highlights: Developed a wireless temperature and humidity monitoring system using ESP32.
Created an Android app to display sensor data in real-time.
Implemented a wireless system connecting patients and doctors via an ad-hoc network.
Designed wearable devices with ESP32 μC and LoRaWAN (RFM95) for wireless communication.
Integrated MCP9808 and MAX30102 sensors for real-time body temperature, heart rate, and SpO2.
Implemented a meshed network using the ClusterDuck Protocol for real-time data collection.
Developed a web-based UI using HTML and JS for monitoring wearable devices.
Authored SEO/SEM content, increasing website search visibility.
Published 'Smart Water Management' article in 'Water Today', showcasing sustainable solutions.

education.institution: New York University, Tandon School of Engineering
courses: Reinforcement Learning & Optimal Control for Robotics

education.institution: Visvesvaraya Technological University, Bangalore Institute of Technology

publications.name: Smart Water Management
publications.summary: Article highlighting sustainable water management solutions.
